classFeature.name: Exploration Knacks
entries: For a ranger exploration isn't just something that happens between destinations - it's a way of life. You start with two ranger {@filter exploration knacks|optionalfeatures|feature type=ek} of your choice. The Knacks Known column of the Ranger table shows when you learn more exploration knacks.

classFeature.name: Familiar Terrain
entries: When making an Intelligence or Wisdom check related to a natural terrain, you gain an expertise die if using a skill you're proficient in. This affects the Chronicle, Gather Components, Harvest, Scout, and Seek Shelter journey activities, but no others. While traveling in a natural terrain, you gain the following benefits:

items: Difficult terrain doesn't slow your group's travel.
You gain an expertise die on Perception checks made to avoid ambushes.
While tracking other creatures, you also learn their exact number, their sizes, and how long ago they passed through the area.

classFeature.name: Expose Weakness
entries: At 1st level, by analysing patterns and drawing from your extensive experience, you gain the ability to expose a creature's weaknesses to you and your allies. Choose one of the following options to determine how you do so.

items.name: Analyse and Study
entries: Whenever you roll for initiative, choose a creature within 60 feet of you and make one of the following ability checks based on its creature type. The DC is equal to 10 + the creature's CR. On a success, the creature is Exposed.
You can use a reaction at the end of each of your turns to repeat the above ability check against a creature within 60 feet of you.

items.name: Observe Defences
entries: Whenever you score a major hit against a creature, you can use your reaction to Expose that creature.

items.name: Observe Offence
entries: Whenever a creature hits you, you can use your reaction to Expose that creature.

Whenever you Expose a creature, you reveal a random weakness it has based on its creature type by rolling a {@dice d8} on one of the following tables. This weakness remains until the creature dies. If you attempt to Expose a creature while a creature is already Exposed by you, the existing Exposed weakness disappears.

classFeature.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to use combat maneuvers. You gain proficiency in two combat traditions from the following list: {@filter Biting Zephyr|spells|Subschool=biting zephyr|}, {@filter Mirror's Glint|spells|Subschool=mirror's glint|}, {@filter Rapid Current|spells|Subschool=rapid current|}, {@filter Razor's Edge|spells|Subschool=razor's edge|}, {@filter Spirited Steed|spells|Subschool=spirited steed|}, and {@filter Unending Wheel|spells|Subschool=unending wheel|}. You can learn two maneuvers of your choice from traditions you are proficient with.

You gain an exertion pool equal to twice your proficiency bonus, regaining any spent exertion at the end of a short or long rest. You use your maneuvers by spending points from your exertion pool. The Manuevers Known column of the Ranger table shows when you learn more maneuvers from a tradition you are proficient with, while the Maneuver Degree column shows the highest degree you can select maneuvers from at a given level.

Additionally, whenever you take a long rest, you can choose any number of the maneuvers you know and replace them with the same number of other maneuvers of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.

classFeature.name: Stride and Seek
entries: Your wilderness survival instincts guide your physical and mental training. You gain one of the following features at 2nd level. You gain the other feature when you reach 5th level in this class.

entries.name: Hunter's Target
entries: You can use your bonus action to focus your attention on a specific enemy. Choose a creature you can see within 90 feet to mark as your quarry. For 1 hour, you are focused on this creature. You gain a +1 bonus to weapon armour attack rolls against the creature, and your weapon armour attacks against it deal an extra {@dice 1d6} damage. In addition, you have advantage on Perception and Survival checks you make to find it. If the creature drops to 0 hit points before the hour ends, you can use a bonus action to mark a new quarry.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Swift Feet
entries: You can use your bonus action to move with greater agility. For 1 minute, your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks. You can choose to end this effect early to either increase your Speed by 30 feet until the end of your turn or gain advantage on a weapon armour attack.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Stride and Seek (2)
entries: You gain the other feature from your Stride and Seek feature.

classFeature.name: Trained Accuracy
entries: At 2nd level, you learn to maintain a level of discipline that spills over into everything you do. This perpetual state of focus allows you to perform incredible feats. You gain both of the following features.

entries.name: Accuracy Bonus
entries: When you make a weapon armour attack, you can use your reaction to gain an attack bonus or damage bonus as shown on the Accuracy Bonus table. Between long rests, you have a number of uses equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1).

entries.name: Accuracy Reserve
entries: If you have not moved since the start of your last turn, you gain a +1 bonus to weapon armour attack rolls during your turn.

classFeature.name: Exploitative
entries: From 3rd level, you become adept at identifying specific weaknesses in your enemies that you and your allies can exploit. Whenever you Expose a creature, you roll two {@dice d8}s instead of one and can choose one of the results rolled for the weakness that is revealed.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Wilderness Mystique
entries: At 4th level your experience as an expert survivalist manifests at the subconscious level. Choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Answering Silence
entries: When others seek your advice on wilderness matters, they often arrive at the correct conclusion despite (or perhaps because of) the silence with which you respond. When a friendly creature makes a Nature or Survival check while adjacent to you, it gains an expertise die. This affects their journey activities.

entries.name: Fearsome Mysticism
entries: Your uncanny abilities and single-minded focus can be unnerving to the uninitiated. You gain an expertise die on Intimidation checks against any creature that is neither proficient in Arcana nor casts spells. This affects the Rob journey activity.

entries.name: Kindred Spirit
entries: You intuitively sense when another wilderness expert is nearby. Your Perception for the purposes of detecting hidden or invisible creatures within 30 feet of you increases by 5, and you instantly detect whether any creature you can see is proficient with Stealth or Survival.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack
entries: From 5th level, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Student of the Hunt
entries: At 6th level, you gain an expertise die on checks made to track non-humanoid creatures, as well as on ability checks to recall information about them. Additionally, you learn one exotic language of your choice.

classFeature.name: Flexible Exposure
entries: At 7th level, you can choose one additional exposure application option from your Expose Weakness feature.

classFeature.name: Explorer's Instinct
entries: At 8th level, once between long rests, you can take two reactions in a round instead of one.

In addition, whenever you learn a new ranger exploration knack or replace an existing one, you can choose from druid {@filter secrets of nature|optionalfeatures|feature type=sn} or fighter {@filter soldiering knacks|optionalfeatures|feature type=sk}.

classFeature.name: Flash of Steel
entries: At 9th level you gain the {@spell Missile Volley|sns} or {@spell Whirlind Strike|sns} combat maneuver. It does not count against your number of maneuvers known and you do not have to spend exertion points to activate it.

classFeature.name: Stride and Seek Upgrade
entries: At 9th level, choose one of the following improvements to a Stride and Seek feature you have.

entries.name: Hunter's Target
entries: You can remain focused on your Hunter's Target for up to 8 hours.

entries.name: Swift Feet
entries: Your Swift Feet now lasts for up to 1 hour.

classFeature.name: Trackless Hunting
entries: At 10th level, you can't be tracked except by magical means, unless you choose to leave a trail.
In addition, whenever you learn a new ranger exploration knack or replace an existing one, you can choose to learn a {@filter rogue skill trick|optionalfeatures|feature type=st} instead.

classFeature.name: Wilderness Lore
entries: At 12th level, your knowledge of wilderness lore deepens. Choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Big Game Traps
entries: Leveraging your skill with snaring small game, you take 10 minutes to set a trap or snare for a creature of Large size or smaller.
The Trap's DC is 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature can spot the trap by making a Perception check against the trap's DC.
Describe the mechanics of your trap to the DM, including what triggers it and what happens when triggered. You may use the following examples or work with the DM to invent your own.
When a creature enters the trapped area or interacts with the trapped object it makes a save or triggers the trap.

items.name: Pendulum Trap
entries: Creatures adjacent to the trap are struck by a swinging object that deals bludeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (determined by the object) equal to 1d8 plus your Wisdom modifier.

items.name: Pit Trap
entries: The creature falls into a pre-existing pit or hazard that you have concealed.

items.name: Rope Trap
entries: The creature is either knocked {@condition prone|sns} or {@condition restrained|sns} by a net or rope.

entries.name: Waste Not
entries: With 10 minutes work you can scavenge valuable cuts from the corpse of a beast, dragon, monstrosity, or plant that has a CR of 1 or higher and has died within the last hour. If sold to a collector, druid, or other naturalist, this notable component is worth an amount of gold equal to 5 x the creature's CR (consult your DM to confirm this gold value).

classFeature.name: Guarded Post
entries: Experience has taught you that defending others is often the key to survival. At 13th level choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Defensive Marksman
entries: When a hostile creature moves towards you or an ally, you can use your reaction to make a ranged weapon attack against it.

entries.name: Hunter's Blind
entries: You can spend 10 minutes constructing a natural shelter able to accommodate you. The shelter blends in with its surroundings, and as long as you don't move into or out of it, the shelter is indistinguishable from a natural object. It is designed to allow you to see out while inside, but prevent anyone outside from seeing in.
The shelter is comfortable and dry, regardless of the weather outside, and counts as a Haven for you. Any other creature that tries to use your Hunter's Blind inadvertantly ruins it after 2d4 hours.

classFeature.name: Tactical Advantage
entries: At 14th level, you can choose one of the following ranger tactics:

entries.name: Covered Shot
entries: When you are protected by half cover, you gain the benefits of three-quarters cover.

entries.name: Nature's Shadow
entries: You gain an expertise die on Stealth checks.

classFeature.name: Survivalcraft
entries: At 16th level, basic survival skills have become second nature to you. Choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Under the Stars
entries: You gain the benefits of a long rest in half the normal amount of time.

entries.name: Woodlore
entries: Using your action, you can produce a flame at a moment's notice even in wet or cold environments. This allows you to ignore the penalties of wet or cold weather on your journey activities. You can hold the flame in your hand without harming yourself or your equipment. Without additional fuel, the flame lasts for 10 minutes, shedding bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

classFeature.name: Hunter's Intent
entries: At 17th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you inflict with a weapon armour attack.

classFeature.name: Flora and Fauna
entries: At 18th level choose one of the following benefits:

entries.name: Beastly Attack
entries: Once on each of your turns, a beast that is friendly to you and able to sense you can use its reaction to make a single basic weapon attack.

entries.name: High Ground
entries: You instantly analyse the natural foliage and features of a given terrain as well as how to leverage them to your advantage. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your walking and climbing speeds increase by 5 feet.
When you are protected by three-quarters cover, you gain the benefits of total cover.
If you make a weapon armour attack against a creature, your movement does not provoke opportunity attacks from it until the beginning of your next turn.

When you move through an area, you can cause it to become difficult terrain until the beginning of your next turn, and any creature that moves through the area during that time has disadvantage on its next attack roll.

classFeature.name: Extreme Exploration
entries: Your travels and training have taken you to new heights and amazing depths. At 19th level, you gain two of the following benefits:

items: You gain 50% resistance to fire and cold damage.
You gain a swimming speed and climbing speed equal to your Speed.

classFeature.name: Nature's Scion
entries: At 20th level, you are a master of yourself and your natural surroundings. You become extremely familiar with all types of terrain and can use the {@optfeature Master Tracker|sns} exploration knack at will.
In addition, you learn Primordial if you don't already know it and gain one of the following:

entries.name: Elemental Mastery
entries: By focusing on a specific natural element within 100 feet (air, fire, earth, water, or wood) you can attract similar essences to you. After 10 minutes of meditation, a creature of that element is drawn to your presence and obeys your commands. This creature is based on a CR 7 generic minion (https://homebrewery.naturalcrit.com/share/4FjimI1VqkgS), but with the following modifications:

items: If the guardian is a fire guardian, its Melee attack deals fire damage. Otherwise, it deals bludgeoning damage.
If the guardian's Melee attack hits a Medium or smaller target, the target is {@condition grappled|sns}, and the guardian uses its Engulf action on it.

The guardian gains the Engulf action: The guardian engulfs a Medium or smaller creature {@condition grappled|sns} by it. The engulfed creature is {@condition blinded|sns}, {@condition restrained|sns}, and unable to breathe, and at the start of each of the guardian's turns it makes a save or suffers {@damage 2d8 + 4} bludgeoning harm (or fire for a fire guardian). If the guardian moves, the engulfed creature moves with it. The guardian can have only one creature engulfed at a time.

The guardian gains the Guard Life action: The guardian uses its Engulf on a willing, {@condition unconscious|sns}, or {@condition incapacitated|sns} ally without dealing damage. While engulfed, the ally is {@condition grappled|sns} but not {@condition restrained|sns}, can see and breathe normally, and has total cover. As soon as the ally takes an action, reaction, or bonus action, they are no longer engulfed. The guardian can release the ally at will (no action required).

entries: The creature disappears after 1 hour, when it is reduced to 0 hit point, or you use a bonus action to dismiss it. Once you have used this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Summon Stampede
entries: You can spend an action speaking directly to the spirit of nature itself to request assistance. All beasts within a 1-mile radius of you are friendly to you for 1 minute, and for the duration on initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), you can choose one of the following effects. You can't use the same effect two rounds in a row. Once you have used this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

items.name: Enraged Fauna
entries: Up to 3 Huge native creatures of up to CR 8 charge forward (use CR 8 generic minions - https://homebrewery.naturalcrit.com/share/4FjimI1VqkgS), bowling into your enemies. Choose any number of creatures you can see within 100 feet to suffer {@dice 9d6} bludgeoning harm. In addition, you can choose one of the Huge creatures to stay and fight. You can have only one such creature assist in this way at a time, and it retreats when bloodied or when you command it to leave.

items.name: Rushing Stampede
entries: A Huge-sized horde of Small, Medium, and Large native creatures hurry through the area, traveling in a 30'-wide line from a direction you choose. Until the start of your next turn, the area is difficult terrain. In addition, each creature of your choice in the area suffers {@dice 5d8} bludgeoning harm. Each target must also make a save, becoming {@condition prone|sns} and {@condition debilitated|sns} until the start of your next turn on a fail. On a successful save, a target is {@condition rattled|sns} until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Swarming Pests
entries: Several Medium-sized swarms of Tiny native creatures enter the spaces of up to 5 Small, Medium, or Large creatures you choose, or the space of a Huge or Gargantuan creature. Each creature in the swarm's area becomes {@condition blinded|sns} and {@condition slowed|sns} until the start of your next turn, and loses concentration on any effect it is concentrating on.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter's Prey
entries: At tier 1, you gain one of the following features of your choice.

items.name: Colossus Slayer
entries: Your tenacity can wear down the most potent foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, the creature takes an extra 1d8 damage if it’s below its hit point maximum. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

items.name: Giant Killer
entries: When a Large or larger creature within 5 feet of you hits or misses you with an attack, you can use your reaction to attack that creature immediately after its attack, provided that you can see the creature.

items.name: Horde Breaker
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make a weapon attack, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within range of your weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter Major Effect #1
entries: All mark damage you deal this combat is increase by 1 die size.

subclassFeature.name: Defensive Tactics
entries: At tier 2, you gain one of the following features of your choice.

items.name: Escape the Horde
entries: Opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage.

items.name: Multiattack Defense
entries: When a creature hits you with an attack, you gain a +4 bonus to AC against all subsequent attacks made by that creature for the rest of the turn.

items.name: Steel Will
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter's Prey
entries: At tier 3, you gain another feature from the Hunter's Prey options from tier 1.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter Major Effect #2
entries: You restore 2 charges of your Trained Accuracy feature.

subclassFeature.name: Multiattack
entries: At tier 4, you gain one of the following features of your choice.

items.name: Volley
entries: You can use your action to make a ranged attack against any number of creatures within 10 feet of a point you can see within your weapon’s range. You must have ammunition for each target, as normal, and you make a separate attack roll for each target.

items.name: Whirlwind Attack
entries: You can use your action to make melee attacks against any number of creatures within your reach, with a separate attack roll for each target.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Hunter's Defence
entries: At tier 5, you gain one of the following features of your choice.

items.name: Evasion
entries: When you are subjected to an effect, such as a red dragon’s fiery breath or a lightning bolt spell, that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on a saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

items.name: Stand Against the Tide
entries: When a hostile creature misses you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to force that creature to repeat the same attack against another creature (other than itself) of your choice.

items.name: Uncanny Dodge
entries: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack’s damage against you.

subclassFeature.name: Dread Ambusher
entries: At tier 1, you master the art of the ambush. You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Wisdom modifier.

During your first turn of each combat, your Speed is increased by 10 feet. If you take the Attack action on that turn, your attack gains one instance of the Empowered Attack feature and deals {@dice 1d8} additional damage of the weapon's base type.

subclassFeature.name: Umbral Sight
entries: At tier 1, you gain 60 feet of darkvision.

You are also adept at evading and hunting creatures that rely on darkvision. While in darkness, you are invisible to any creature that relies on darkvision to see you in that darkness. Additionally, whenever you Expose a creature with darkvision, you can modify the final roll on the weakness table by 1.

subclassFeature.name: Gloom Stalker Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Gloom Stalker Rangers have access to the following major effect options:

items: Your weapon damage increases by an amount equal to half your level (rounded up), and does so again whenever you fail to hit with a weapon attack. This effect ends and the bonus damage is lost the next time you score a major success with a weapon attack, after that attack deals damage.

You may move up one space in the initiative tracker. Then, you gain a bonus to weapon damage against creatures behind you in the initiative tracker for the remainder of combat equal to half your level (rounded up).

subclassFeature.name: Creeping Doom
entries: At tier 2, when you Expose a creature, you may cause it to project a 5-foot-radius sphere of darkness around itself until the Exposure ends.

subclassFeature.name: Precision in Shadow
entries: At tier 3, whenever you score a critical failure or minor success with a weapon attack against a creature in darkness, the attack deals damage as if it were a standard success.

subclassFeature.name: Dark Veil
entries: At tier 4, your reflexes sharpen when battle begins. During the first round of combat, you have 50% resistance to damage and add half your Wisdom modifier to your armour. Additionally, whenever you take damage during that round, you may immediately use your reaction to shift up to half your Speed.

subclassFeature.name: Eclipsed Deflection
entries: At tier 5, shadows bend instinctively to shield you from harm. Whenever you take damage from an attack made by a creature that is in darkness, you reduce the damage by an amount equal to your Wisdom modifier + half your level. Additionally, you also impose disadvantage on the next attack roll the attacker makes.

optionalfeature.name: Beast Friend
entries: After a Befriend Animal journey activity, you can befriend and feed a Medium or smaller beast of your choice, becoming its guardian ranger (use the CR 1/8 generic minion - https://homebrewery.naturalcrit.com/share/4FjimI1VqkgS). It counts as a minion and requires your concentration, but you cannot lose this concentration against your will. The beast won't attack except as a response to being attacked, but it can take other actions as normal and makes death saving throws. You can only be a guardian ranger to one beast at a time.

optionalfeature.name: Build Shelter
entries: You can change a failure on a Seek Shelter journey activity check into a success.
Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again for a week.

optionalfeature.name: Calls of the Wild
entries: Using verbal and nonverbal cues to communicate with beasts, you can gain basic information from them or make a simple request (like delivering a shrot message or small object).

optionalfeature.name: Ear to the Ground
entries: By expending 1 minute remaining stationary, using sound a vibrations alone, until you move or take an action you are able to deduce information for up to 30' in every direction, as if using blindsight or tremorsense.

optionalfeature.name: Expert Foraging
entries: After a Harvest journey activity, you can collect a number of edible, non-poisonous plants and grubs equal to your proficiency bonus. A creature can use its action to eat up to 5 plants or grubs, each of which restores 1 hit point. A creature who eats at least 1 also recovers 1 level of fatigue, but can only benefit from this once per long rest. The plants and grubs die and spoil 1 hour after being collected.

optionalfeature.name: Forced Marcher
entries: The journeys your party undertake are reduced in length by 1 day, to a minimum of 1 day.

optionalfeature.name: Guide
entries: While traveling, when you would have a failure for a Scout journey activity, you succeed instead, and you can Scout in addition to engaging in any other journey activity.

optionalfeature.name: Healing Salves
entries: On a success or higher on a Harvest journey activity, you can collect natural ingredients and concoct an ointment that speeds healing. This salve is potent for 24 hours and has a number of uses equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier. As an action, you can use the salve to restore {@dice 1d4} hit points to a creature within reach.

optionalfeature.name: Herbal Bitters
entries: On a success or higher on a Harvest journey activity, you can collect ingredients and brew two servings of a bitter drink that is potent for 24 hours. Imbibing a serving takes an action and reduces fatigue by one level for 4 hours.

optionalfeature.name: Huntsman
entries: You gain advantage on Gather Component journey activity checks.

optionalfeature.name: Longwalker
entries: When you would gain fatigue during a journey, you gain one fewer level of fatigue. Alternatively, you can choose to accept the level of fatigue, allowing up to two traveling companions to forgo gaining a level of fatigue instead. Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again for a week.

optionalfeature.name: Master Tracker
entries: You can spend 5 minutes looking at a 30' diameter outdoor area to study the ground and other clues. You learn all events which transpired in that area in the last 24 hours as though you had witnessed them yourself, although you do not learn secret information not obvious to an observer. The information you gain is only that which would be learned by sight; you do not gain sound-based information or learn what creatures in the area said to each other.
Once you have used this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Monster Mimic
entries: By imitating how a non-humanoid creature sounds, looks, and moves, you gain advantage on Deception and Intimidation checks made to convince a creature that it is seeing or hearing that beast.

optionalfeature.name: Poisons and Antidotes
entries: After a Gather Components journey activity, you can extract 3 doses of liquid poison (when ingested, a creature makes a save or is {@condition poisoned|sns} for 1 hour) from poisonous plants you've foraged. Alternatively, using the same plants you can spend an hour making a dose of antitoxin.

optionalfeature.name: Read the Room
entries: By spending 10 minutes observing facial expressions, body language, and vocal intonation, you can detect minute details which give you insight into the creatures within 30' of you. For the next minute, you can direct your focus on any one creature that you can see within 30'. If the creature you choose has an Intelligence of 3 or lower or doesn't speak any language, the creature is unaffected.
You learn the surface thoughts of the creature - what is most on its mind in that moment. As an action, you can shift your attention to another creature's thoughts.

optionalfeature.name: Relentless Pursuit
entries: Once you have successfully picked up the tracks of a creature, you are able to follow those tracks without any need for further checks for a period of up to 7 days.

optionalfeature.name: See the Unseen
entries: Once between long rests you can focus your attention on peripheral sights, sounds, and other signs of unseen passage. For the next hour, you can detect {@condition invisible|sns} creatures and objects, their location, movement, and actions, and even sense creatures and objects in the Ethereal Plane.

optionalfeature.name: Survivalist
entries: When traveling you may undertake two journey activities per day instead of one. Both activities must be from the following list: Befriend Creature, Gather Components, Harvest, Scout.

optionalfeature.name: Foreign Interference
entries: The next time the aberration suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it loses the ability to score major successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Telepathic Presence
entries: Whenever a creature within 60 feet of the aberration gains any of the {@condition charmed|sns}, {@condition dominated|sns}, {@condition fixated|sns}, {@condition frightened|sns}, {@condition rattled|sns}, {@condition strife|sns}, or {@condition taunted|sns} conditions, the aberration must make a save. On a fail, it gains the same condition until it passes a passive save.

optionalfeature.name: Photonic Power
entries: Whenever the aberration targets a creature wearing metal armour or wielding a shield with a damaging attack, the target can use its reaction to partially reflect the attack, reducing the attack's success tier by 1. If a melee or ranged attack is reflected, the aberration also suffers the effects that this tier reduction removed from the attack. For example, a major success being reduced to a success will reflect the major effect, but a standard success becoming a minor success will reflect half the attack's damage.

optionalfeature.name: Bizarre Nature
entries: The next time the aberration is healed by you or an ally, it loses the ability to score major and critical successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; such successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Psychic Agnosia
entries: While an illusion spell is active within the aberration's line of sight, the aberration is {@condition confused|sns} and includes itself in options for the condition's free attack.

optionalfeature.name: Cracked Exterior
entries: Whenever the aberration suffers a major hit that deals physical base damage, the attacker can halve the attack's damage to reduce the aberration's armour by 1 for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Inferior Constitution
entries: While the aberration has any of the {@condition debilitated|sns}, {@condition fatigue|sns}, {@condition poisoned|sns}, {@condition sluggish|sns}, or {@condition staggered|sns} conditions, it loses a number of hit points at the start of each of its turns equal to quadruple your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Photophobia
entries: Whenever the aberration changes from being within darkness to bright light, it uses its reaction to move up to its Speed away from the light source. Additionally, opportunity attacks have advantage against it when it is moving in this way.

optionalfeature.name: Tunnel Vision
entries: The beast has 50% vulnerability to the damage of melee attacks from creatures flanking it.

optionalfeature.name: Susceptible
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 30 feet of the beast can make a Nature check with DC equal to 10 + the beast's CR. On a success, the beast is {@condition dominated|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Energy Vulnerability
entries: The next time the beast suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it becomes {@condition slowed|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Notable Smell
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 30 feet of the beast can make a Perception check with DC equal to 10 + the beast's CR. On a success, the beast's attacks have disadvantage against that creature for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Taste for Flesh
entries: The beast is always {@condition taunted|sns} by your ally (including yourself) wearing the lightest body armour type. If multiple creatures meet this condition, the beast is taunted by all of them.

optionalfeature.name: Righting Inability
entries: Whenever the beast is knocked {@condition prone|sns}, it cannot stand up until it uses an action to do so. Additionally, it does not impose disadvantage on non-adjacent armour attacks while {@condition prone|sns}.

optionalfeature.name: Acquisitive
entries: The beast is always {@condition taunted|sns} by your ally (including yourself) holding the most gold. If multiple creatures meet this condition, the beast is taunted by all of them.

optionalfeature.name: Weak Joints
entries: c

optionalfeature.name: Empathic Manifestation
entries: The next time an ally of the celestial dies within 120 feet of it, the celestial gains 50% vulnerability to physical damage for the remainder of combat. If this effect is not active and the celestial has no allies within 120 feet of it, physical attacks against it instead deal additional base damage equal to the celestial's CR.

optionalfeature.name: Relentless Pursuit
entries: The celestial is always {@condition taunted|sns} by your ally (including yourself) that it believes to have committed the most evil deed within the last century. It does not learn what this deed is.

optionalfeature.name: Egotistical Pride
entries: While the celestial is {@condition prone|sns}, it has 50% vulnerability to physical damage and incoming attacks against it have +1 critical success range.

optionalfeature.name: Heavenly Purity
entries: The celestial is always {@condition taunted|sns} by your ally (including yourself) that last dealt non-physical (except radiant) damage to it.

optionalfeature.name: Bound by Prayer
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 30 feet of the celestial can make a History check with DC equal to 10 + the celestial's CR. On a success, the celestial becomes {@condition slowed|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Decorative Garb
entries: A creature can target the celestial with the {@spell grab on|sns} basic maneuver, regardless of size difference. Only one creature can grab onto the celestial in this way at a time, and while doing so, the celestial cannot make attacks against the grabber unless it rolls over to do so, has disadvantage on all attacks, and deals double damage to its grabber if it manages to dislodge them.

optionalfeature.name: Covenant of Truth
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 30 feet of the celestial can knowingly speak a lie, making a Deception check with DC equal to 10 + the celestial's CR. On a failure, the celestial's armour is reduced by 3 for the remainder of combat. This effect does not stack.

optionalfeature.name: Energy Overload
entries: Whenever the celestial takes non-physical damage, it emits a burst of power, granting you and allies within 10 feet of it a number of temporary hit points equal to its CR plus your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Programmed Attack Patterns
entries: The construct has disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures that are proficient with all weapons the construct is wielding (if it is unarmed, it counts as wielding Brass Knuckles).

optionalfeature.name: Exploitable
entries: As a bonus action, a creature adjacent to the construct can make a Magiscience check with DC equal to 10 + the construct's CR. On a success, the construct gains 50% vulnerability to damage for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Flawed Construction
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 30 feet of the construct can make a Perception check with DC equal to 10 + the construct's CR. On a success, that creature's attacks against the construct have +5 critical success range for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Puppetable
entries: The next time the construct is directly affected by a transmutation spell, it becomes {@condition dominated|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Energy Disruption
entries: The next time the construct suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it loses the ability to score major successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Programmed Movements
entries: Creatures proficient in medium or heavy armour have advantage on attack rolls against the construct.

optionalfeature.name: Sentinel Configuration
entries: The construct's intercept range increases to 30 feet, and whenever it has the opportunity to intercept an attack against one of its allies, it does so. Additionally, whenever the construct intercepts an attack, its armour is reduced by 1 for the remainder of combat. While the construct has no allies within 30 feet of it, armour attacks against it have +1 critical success range.

optionalfeature.name: Weak Bindings
entries: The next time a {@filter spell with the negation subschool|spells|Subschool=negation} is cast on the construct, or its area includes the construct, the construct becomes {@condition restrained|sns} by the caster and {@condition brittle|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Obsessed with Control
entries: While the dragon is {@condition charmed|sns}, {@condition dominated|sns}, {@condition fixated|sns}, {@condition frightened|sns}, or {@condition taunted|sns}, it loses a number of hit points at the start of each of its turns equal to quadruple your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Lingering Potency
entries: Whenever the dragon deals physical damage, it gains 50% vulnerability to the next instance of the same damage type from a weapon attack before its next turn. Whenever it deals non-physical damage, it gains similar vulnerability but for spell attacks instead.

optionalfeature.name: Intense Polarity
entries: The next time the dragon suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage of a type that the dragon cannot deal itself, it becomes {@condition restrained|sns} by its attacker and {@condition slowed|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Insular
entries: Whenever the dragon suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage of a type that the dragon cannot deal itself, it becomes {@condition frightened|sns} of the attacker for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Long Neck
entries: While the dragon is {@condition grappled|sns}, it cannot score major successes with its attacks; its major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Cracked Scales
entries: Whenever the dragon suffers a major hit that deals physical base damage of a type that the dragon cannot deal itself, that hit's base damage is added again as true damage.

optionalfeature.name: Fragile Polarity
entries: The next time the dragon suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage of a type that the dragon cannot deal itself, it loses the ability to score major and critical successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major and critical successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Blindspot
entries: Creatures with height advantage against the dragon have advantage on their attack rolls against it.

optionalfeature.name: Grounding Glyph
entries: When this weakness is first exposed, you identify a glyph located on a random space near the elemental. The glyph fits into the space as required by its non-creature contents, such as being on a wall's surface, the bottom of a table, or floating in space. This space is determined by rolling a {@dice d8} and a {@dice d12}. The {@dice d12} shows the number of 5-foot spaces away from the elemental the space is, in a cardinal direction defined by the {@dice d8} (1 is north, 2 is northeast, 3 is east, and so on in clockwise order). A creature can use a bonus action on the glyph's space to immediately cause the elemental to lose hit points equal to 6 times your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Fragile Attunement
entries: The next time the elemental suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage of a type that the elemental cannot deal itself, it loses the ability to score major and critical successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major and critical successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Merinthophobia
entries: While the elemental is {@condition grappled|sns}, it is also {@condition restrained|sns} and {@condition frightened|sns} of its grappler.

optionalfeature.name: Weak Bonds
entries: The next time the elemental suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage of a type that the elemental cannot deal itself, it becomes {@condition restrained|sns} by the attacker and {@condition slowed|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Unstable Presence
entries: The elemental's concentration is occupied by this weakness for the remainder of combat. Whenever its concentration would otherwise be broken, it loses hit points equal to quadruple your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Delicate
entries: The next time the elemental suffers a major hit that deals physical base damage of a type that the elemental cannot deal itself, it gains 50% vulnerability to physical damage for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Hypertangible
entries: Whenever the elemental suffers a major hit that deals physical base damage of a type that the elemental cannot deal itself, that hit's base damage is added again as true damage.

optionalfeature.name: Feeble Core
entries: The elemental has disadvantage with attack rolls against creatures wearing medium or heavy armour.

optionalfeature.name: Occult Reality
entries: While an illusion spell is active within the fey's line of sight, the fey has disadvantage on attack rolls.

optionalfeature.name: Sacred Sprout
entries: When this weakness is first exposed, a tiny plant resembling the fey sprouts in a random space near the fey. The plant fits into the space as required by its non-creature contents, such as growing out of a wall's surface, a table's top, or floating in space. This space is determined by rolling a {@dice d8} and a {@dice d12}. The {@dice d12} shows the number of 5-foot spaces away from the fey the space is, in a cardinal direction defined by the {@dice d8} (1 is north, 2 is northeast, 3 is east, and so on in clockwise order). A creature can use a bonus action on the plant's space to immediately cause the fey to lose hit points equal to 6 times your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Twisted by Words
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 30 feet of the fey can make a Persuasion check with DC equal to 10 + the fey's CR. On a success, the fey becomes {@condition poisoned|sns} for the remainder of combat, and fortitude attacks against it have advantage for the same duration.

optionalfeature.name: Entitled Freedom
entries: While the fey is {@condition grappled|sns}, it is also {@condition dazed|sns}.

optionalfeature.name: Choreomania
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 30 feet of the fey can start singing, making a Charisma check with DC equal to 6 + the fey's CR. On a success, the fey cannot stop itself from dancing for the remainder of combat. While it is dancing, all spell attacks against it have advantage.

optionalfeature.name: Self-Blindness
entries: Creatures wearing metal armour or wielding a shield are considered invisible to the fey.

optionalfeature.name: Gilded Pact
entries: As a bonus action, a creature adjacent to the fey may gift it a gold piece, causing it to be {@condition dominated|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Bound to Reflections
entries: While the fey is within 10 feet of a source of dim or brighter light, a creature also within that area can use its bonus action to force the fey to repeat its last movement exactly. If this movement would cause it to collide with a small or larger creature, object, or structure, it stops and takes damage as if it were being pushed.

optionalfeature.name: Dessicated Flesh
entries: Whenever the fiend is directly affected by a {@filter spell with the air subschool|spells|Subschool=air}, it loses hit points equal to quadruple your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Fuelled by Fury
entries: The fiend has a -3 penalty to its attack rolls for the remainder of combat. Whenever a major success is scored against the fiend, it gains a +1 bonus to attack rolls for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Reckless
entries: Whenever the fiend fails to hit a creature taking the Block action with any damaging attack, the attack targets itself instead, using the same final attack roll result but increasing the attack's success tier by 1.

optionalfeature.name: Melophobia
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 30 feet of the fiend can start singing, making a Charisma check with DC equal to 6 + the fiend's CR. On a success, the fiend has disadvantage on attack rolls against the singer and creatures adjacent to the singer for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: See No Evil
entries: The fiend cannot score major or critical successes with its attacks against creatures with their eyes closed; such attacks are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Looking for a Challenge
entries: The fiend has disadvantage with attacks against creatures with a condition.

optionalfeature.name: Energy Sensitivity
entries: The next time the fiend suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it loses the ability to score major successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Unholy Nature
entries: As a bonus action, a creature can say a silent prayer, making a History check with DC equal to 10 + the fiend's CR. On a success, that creature gains 50% resistance to the fiend's damage for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Analysis Paralysis
entries: The giant has disadvantage on attack rolls while at least three hostile Medium or smaller creatures are adjacent to it.

optionalfeature.name: All Bluster
entries: The giant has disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures with at least 3 will.

optionalfeature.name: Fragile Origin
entries: Whenever the giant suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage of a type that the giant cannot deal itself, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against the attacker for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Muscular Pride
entries: The giant is always {@condition taunted|sns} by your ally (including yourself) with the highest Strength score.

optionalfeature.name: Partial to Pretty Lights
entries: The next time a {@filter spell with the prismatic subschool|spells|Subschool=prismatic} produces an effect within 60 feet of the giant and within its line of sight, the giant becomes {@condition dominated|sns} by the caster for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Humanoid Allergy
entries: Whenever the giant starts its turn adjacent to a humanoid, it must make a save. On a fail, the giant's concentration is occupied by this weakness for the remainder of combat. Whenever its concentration would otherwise be broken during this effect, it loses hit points equal to 6 times your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Toxic Emotions
entries: While the giant is {@condition charmed|sns}, {@condition dominated|sns}, {@condition fixated|sns}, {@condition frightened|sns}, or {@condition taunted|sns}, it is also {@condition weakened|sns}.

optionalfeature.name: Allometry
entries: While the giant has any of the {@condition debilitated|sns}, {@condition fatigue|sns}, {@condition poisoned|sns}, {@condition sluggish|sns}, or {@condition staggered|sns} conditions, it cannot score major successes with its attacks; such major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Clumsy
entries: Whenever the humanoid targets a creature wearing metal armour or wielding a shield with a damaging attack, the target can use its reaction to partially reflect the attack, reducing the attack's success tier by 1. If a melee or ranged attack is reflected, the humanoid also suffers the effects that this tier reduction removed from the attack. For example, a major success being reduced to a success will reflect the major effect, but a standard success becoming a minor success will reflect half the attack's damage.

optionalfeature.name: Unfamiliar Footing
entries: Attack rolls have advantage against the humanoid while it is standing in difficult terrain.

optionalfeature.name: Vulnerable to Greater Forces
entries: The next time the humanoid suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it loses the ability to score major successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Poor Attention Span
entries: The humanoid is always {@condition taunted|sns} by the creature that last attacked it.

optionalfeature.name: Shellshock
entries: The next time the humanoid suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it becomes {@condition restrained|sns} by the attacker for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Fragile Mind
entries: While the humanoid has any of the {@condition charmed|sns}, {@condition dominated|sns}, {@condition fixated|sns}, {@condition frightened|sns}, {@condition rattled|sns}, {@condition strife|sns}, or {@condition taunted|sns} conditions, it gains 50% vulnerability to non-physical damage.

optionalfeature.name: Pinned Down
entries: While the humanoid is adjacent to at least two hostile creatures, it loses the ability to score major successes with its attacks; such major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Left Exposed
entries: Whenever the humanoid makes an attack roll with disadvantage, the next attack roll against it has advantage.

optionalfeature.name: Targetable Weapon
entries: Whenever a creature makes a damaging melee attack against the monstrosity, the attacker can impose disadvantage on its attack roll. If the attack still hits, the monstrosity loses the ability to score major and critical successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major and critical successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Vulnerable Joints
entries: The next time the monstrosity suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it becomes {@condition restrained|sns} by the attacker for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Violent Compulsions
entries: While the monstrosity is {@condition charmed|sns}, it is also {@condition dominated|sns} by the charmer. Additionally, whenever the monstrosity is forced by another creature to make an attack as a free action, that attack roll has advantage.

optionalfeature.name: Ravenous Hunger
entries: While adjacent to a corpse or {@condition prone|sns} creature and within 30 feet of the monstrosity, a creature can use its bonus action to make a Persuasion check with DC equal to 10 + the monstrosity's CR. On a success, the monstrosity is {@condition taunted|sns} by the corpse or {@condition prone|sns} creature until the monstrosity is hurt or the {@condition prone|sns} creature stands up.

optionalfeature.name: Easy Quarry
entries: The monstrosity gains 50% vulnerability to ranged weapon damage for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Telegraphed Attacks
entries: Whenever the monstrosity fails to hit a creature taking the Block action with any damaging attack, the target can use its reaction to make an attack against the monstrosity that only targets the monstrosity (range restrictions still apply). The monstrosity has 50% vulnerability to such an attack.

optionalfeature.name: Sciophobia
entries: Whenever the monstrosity changes from being within bright light to darkness, it becomes {@condition frightened|sns} of you and {@condition brittle|sns} until it re-enters bright light.

optionalfeature.name: Exposed Weapons
entries: The next time the monstrosity suffers a major hit that deals physical base damage, it loses the ability to score major successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Explosive Reaction
entries: Whenever a {@filter spell with the water subschool|spells|Subschool=water} is cast on the ooze, or its area includes the ooze, the ooze loses hit points equal to 8 times your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Stunted Kinetics
entries: Whenever the ooze suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against the attacker for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Immiscible
entries: The next time a {@filter spell with the earth subschool|spells|Subschool=earth} is cast on the ooze, or its area includes the ooze, the ooze loses the ability to score major successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Reactive Cohesion
entries: The next time the ooze suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, if there is another ooze within 60 feet of it, it immediately is absorbed into the closest one, increasing the other ooze's size by one step, adding its current hit points to the other ooze's total, and being effectively destroyed. If there is not another ooze within 60 feet, the ooze instead becomes {@condition weakened|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Solvent Shock
entries: The next time the ooze suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it becomes {@condition slowed|sns} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Energy Brittling
entries: The next time the ooze suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, it gains 50% vulnerability to non-physical damage for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Disrupted Viscosity
entries: Whenever the ooze is pushed or knocked {@condition prone|sns}, it loses hit points equal to quadruple your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Osmotic Drain
entries: Whenever the ooze changes from being within darkness to bright light, it becomes {@condition debilitated|sns} until it returns to darkness.

optionalfeature.name: Vulnerable to Pests
entries: The plant gains 50% vulnerability to the damage of minions and {@filter spells with the beasts subschool|spells|Subschool=beasts} for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Genetic Weakness
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 30 feet of the plant can make a Nature check with DC equal to 10 + the plant's CR. On a success, the plant gains 50% vulnerability to non-physical damage for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Sustaining Root
entries: When this weakness is first exposed, a root emerges in a random space near the plant. The root fits into the space as required by its non-creature contents, such as growing out of a wall's surface, a table's top, or folds in empty space. This space is determined by rolling a {@dice d8} and a {@dice d12}. The {@dice d12} shows the number of 5-foot spaces away from the plant the space is, in a cardinal direction defined by the {@dice d8} (1 is north, 2 is northeast, 3 is east, and so on in clockwise order). A creature can use a bonus action on the root's space to remove the plant's ability to score major successes with its attacks for 1 round; such major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Lengthy Extrusions
entries: The plant has disadvantage with attack rolls against Small or smaller and {@condition prone|sns} creatures.

optionalfeature.name: Epiphyte
entries: The next time an ally of the plant dies within 120 feet of it, the plant becomes {@condition frightened|sns} of the creature responsible for the ally's death for the remainder of combat. If this effect is not active and the plant has no allies within 120 feet of it, it is instead {@condition rattled|sns} and {@condition sluggish|sns}.

optionalfeature.name: Exposed Roots
entries: The plant gains 50% vulnerability to the damage of armour attacks from adjacent creatures.

optionalfeature.name: Poor Mobility
entries: Fortitude and reflex attack rolls have advantage against the plant.

optionalfeature.name: Thriving Instincts
entries: The next time the plant is healed by you or an ally, it becomes {@condition dominated|sns} by the healer for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Brittle Body
entries: The undead gains 50% vulnerability to critical damage.

optionalfeature.name: Trauma-Bound
entries: A creature within 60 feet of the undead can use a bonus action to attempt to convince the undead that its pain and trauma is understood, making a contested Deception check against the undead's Insight. If the undead loses this contest, it loses the ability to score major successes with its attacks for the remainder of combat; its major successes are instead counted as standard successes.

optionalfeature.name: Vulnerable Head
entries: Whenever a creature makes a damaging armour attack against the monstrosity, the attacker can impose disadvantage on its attack roll. If they do so, the attack's final base damage is added again as true damage.

optionalfeature.name: Life Snuffer
entries: The undead is always {@condition taunted|sns} by your ally (including yourself) with the most current hit points.

optionalfeature.name: Remnants of the Past
entries: When the undead dies, it awards one additional loot.

optionalfeature.name: Leaking Animation Magic
entries: Whenever the undead suffers a major hit that deals non-physical base damage, the attacker rolls on the wild magic table (TEMP while no S&S version available: http://dnd2024.wikidot.com/sorcerer:wild-magic-sorcery).

optionalfeature.name: Unholy Protection
entries: As a bonus action, a creature within 60 feet of the undead can say a silent prayer, making a History check with DC equal to 10 + the undead's CR. On a success, spell attack rolls have advantage against the undead for the remainder of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Attachment to Surroundings
entries: Damage against the undead is tripled from improvised weapons that were within 60 feet of the undead when this combat started.
